workout.title: Chest Day - BODYBUILD
workout.description: Chest Day workout focusing on bodybuild training.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight natural arch in the low back.
Grip the bar with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width, wrists stacked over elbows and thumbs wrapped around the bar.
Retract and depress the shoulder blades (pinch the shoulder blades together) to create a stable chest platform.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar over the chest so it is balanced and steady.
Inhale, lower the bar under control to the mid-chest (sternum) keeping elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso.
Pause briefly with the bar touching or just above the chest while maintaining tightness through the core and legs.
Exhale and drive the bar upward by extending the elbows, pressing the bar back to the start position while keeping wrists neutral.
Avoid excessive elbow flare or bouncing the bar off the chest; maintain a controlled tempo and keep feet planted.
After the set, carefully re-rack the bar by guiding it back onto the bench supports before releasing your grip.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a supine pressing exercise performed on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also working the triceps and anterior delts. The lifter lowers a barbell to the chest and presses it back to full arm extension under control.

exercises.name: Barbell Incline Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench at about 30–45 degrees and position it in a rack with safety catches or a spotter available.
instructions: Set an incline bench at about 30–45 degrees and position it in a rack with safety catches or a spotter available.
Lie supine on the incline bench with your head supported, feet flat on the floor, and back slightly arched while maintaining scapular retraction.
Grip the barbell with a pronated grip slightly wider than shoulder-width; wrists stacked over elbows.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar over your upper chest/shoulder line, then stabilize before starting the descent.
Inhale and slowly lower the bar in a controlled path to the upper chest/clavicular area, keeping elbows at roughly a 30–60° angle from the torso (avoid flaring to 90°).
Lightly touch or stop just above the chest, pause briefly without bouncing, then exhale and press the bar upward and slightly back to the start position, fully extending the elbows.
Keep core braced and feet planted throughout to maintain stability and prevent excessive lower back extension.
Carefully re-rack the bar under control or have a spotter assist when finished.
exercises.description: A barbell press performed on an incline bench that emphasizes the upper portion of the chest by pressing the bar upward and slightly toward the clavicle. It also engages the front delts and triceps for pressing and locking out the weight.

exercises.name: Cable Bent Over Single Arm Crossover
exercises.notes: Set a single handle on a cable pulley at about shoulder to chest height and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Set a single handle on a cable pulley at about shoulder to chest height and select an appropriate weight.

Stand to the side of the pulley with a staggered or shoulder-width stance and hinge forward at the hips until your torso is roughly 30–45° from vertical (slight bend in knees); maintain a neutral spine and brace your abs.
Grab the handle with the hand farthest from the machine, arm extended out to the side and palm facing down or slightly inward; allow a soft bend in the elbow.

Keep your shoulder down and stable, then exhale and pull the handle in a horizontal arc across your body toward the center of your chest, leading with the elbow and squeezing the pectorals at the end range.
Pause briefly with the chest contracted, keeping the torso still (avoid rotating the ribs or shoulders), then inhale and slowly reverse the motion to return the hand to the start under control.
Perform the desired reps, then switch sides and repeat. Maintain wrist neutrality and controlled tempo throughout.
exercises.description: A single-arm cable movement performed from a bent-over stance that sweeps the working arm across the body to load and compress the chest fibers, emphasizing the inner and upper pectoral region while the torso is stabilized.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.notes: Light weight, focus on form and range of motion

warmup.notes: Brief warmup to activate target muscles. Can skip if first working set feels warm.

workout.title: Chest Day - BODYBUILD
workout.description: Chest Day workout focusing on bodybuild training.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set up a flat bench inside a rack so the barbell sits directly above your eyes when you are lying down.
instructions: Set up a flat bench inside a rack so the barbell sits directly above your eyes when you are lying down.

Lie supine on the bench with your head, upper back and glutes in contact with the bench, feet flat on the floor and a small natural arch in the lower back; retract and depress your shoulder blades to create a stable chest platform.
Grip the barbell with a pronated (overhand) grip slightly wider than shoulder-width; wrists should be stacked over the elbows and forearms vertical when the bar is over the chest.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar out over the chest while keeping the scapulae stable; lock the elbows briefly before starting the set.
Inhale and lower the bar in a controlled manner to the mid-chest/sternum (around nipple level), keeping the elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso to protect the shoulders.
Allow the bar to touch or come very close to the chest without bouncing; maintain tension in the pectorals and keep the feet and glutes braced.
Exhale and press the bar upward explosively while keeping the bar path slightly arced toward the hips until the arms are fully extended but not hyperextended.
Pause briefly at the top with the shoulders stable, then begin the next rep. Maintain consistent form and breathing throughout the set.
Use a spotter or safety pins in the rack for heavy sets, and avoid flaring the elbows excessively or letting the lower back lift off the bench.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a compound pressing movement performed lying supine on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also recruiting the triceps and anterior delts. It involves lowering a loaded barbell to the chest and pressing it back up to full arm extension with controlled technique.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Alternative Fly
exercises.notes: Set an adjustable bench to a 30–45° incline and sit with a dumbbell in each hand.
instructions: Set an adjustable bench to a 30–45° incline and sit with a dumbbell in each hand. Plant your feet flat on the floor and lie back so your head, upper back, and butt are supported (supine on incline bench).
Hold the dumbbells with a neutral or slightly pronated grip and press them together over your upper chest with a small bend in both elbows; this is the start position.
Keep your shoulder blades lightly retracted and braced throughout the set to protect the shoulder joint.

Lower one dumbbell in a slow, controlled arc out to the side (maintain the same elbow bend) until you feel a stretch in the upper chest; keep the opposite arm stationary and slightly elevated above the chest.
Pause briefly at the bottom for tension on the pectorals, then drive that dumbbell back up in the same arc to meet the elevated dumbbell above the chest, squeezing the pectorals at the top.
Alternate sides each repetition, maintaining control, a constant elbow angle, and avoiding bouncing or letting the lowered elbow drop behind the plane of your torso.
Breathe in as you lower the weight and exhale as you bring it back up. Use a weight that allows strict form and full control for the full range of motion.
Avoid shrugging the shoulders or overarching the lower back; stop immediately if you feel sharp shoulder pain and reduce range or load.
exercises.description: Lying supine on an incline bench, perform alternating single-arm dumbbell flyes in a wide arc to isolate the upper chest (pectorals) while one arm remains elevated as the other lowers. The movement emphasizes a controlled stretch and contraction of the upper chest with stabilization from the anterior delts and forearms.

exercises.name: Lever Incline Chest Press
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat so that the handles are aligned with the upper chest/ clavicle area when seated and set an appropriate weight on the lever.
instructions: Adjust the seat so that the handles are aligned with the upper chest/ clavicle area when seated and set an appropriate weight on the lever.
Sit with your back and head pressed firmly against the incline pad, feet flat on the floor or platform for stability.
Reach up and grasp the handles with a neutral or slightly overhand grip, elbows bent and positioned slightly below shoulder height at the start.
Brace your core and exhale as you press the handles upward and slightly together until your arms are nearly extended; avoid forcefully locking the elbows.
Pause briefly at the top without shrugging shoulders or arching the lower back.
Inhale and slowly control the handles back down to the starting position with elbows tracking under the wrists and chest muscles lengthening.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions while maintaining full contact between your back/head and the pad and keeping movement smooth and controlled.
If you feel excessive shoulder strain, reduce range of motion or weight and ensure the seat/handle alignment is correct.
exercises.description: Seated incline lever chest press performed on an incline pressing machine that targets the upper portion of the chest by pressing the handles upward and slightly together from chest level. The back remains supported on the inclined pad while the shoulders and triceps assist the movement.

exercises.name: Barbell Wide Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set up a flat bench under a rack and load the barbell; position yourself lying supine on the bench with eyes roughly under the bar.
instructions: Set up a flat bench under a rack and load the barbell; position yourself lying supine on the bench with eyes roughly under the bar.
Plant feet firmly on the floor, pull your shoulder blades together and down (retract and depress) and maintain a tight core and neutral spine.
Grip the bar with a wide, pronated grip (wider than shoulder-width) so the forearms are more angled when the bar is at chest level.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar over your chest, keeping shoulders stable and the elbows under the bar.
Inhale, slowly lower the bar in a controlled manner to the mid-chest / nipple line while keeping the elbows tracking out at a moderate flare and the scapulae retracted.
Pause briefly with the bar lightly touching the chest (do not bounce), then exhale and press the bar back up along a slight arc until the arms are nearly extended.
Avoid locking the elbows aggressively at the top, maintain tension in the pectorals and triceps, and keep the wrists neutral.
Return the bar to the rack with control after your set; use a spotter if lifting heavy or approaching failure.
exercises.description: A barbell bench press performed with a wider-than-shoulder grip to emphasize the outer pectoral fibers. Lying supine on a flat bench, the bar is lowered to the mid-chest and pressed back to full arm extension.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Fly
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor and lower back in a neutral position.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor and lower back in a neutral position.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral or slightly pronated grip and extend the arms straight above your chest, palms facing each other.
Keep a slight, fixed bend in the elbows throughout the movement (do not lock the elbows).
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc, maintaining the slight elbow bend until you feel a stretch in the chest or your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor.
Keep the shoulder blades stable on the bench (no excessive shrugging) and avoid letting the dumbbells travel behind the plane of your torso.
Pause briefly at the bottom under control, then exhale and bring the dumbbells back up along the same arc, focusing on squeezing the pectorals until the weights meet above the sternum.
Perform each rep with controlled tempo and avoid bouncing or using momentum; select a weight that allows full control and a safe range of motion.
exercises.description: A supine resistance exercise performed with dumbbells that isolates the chest by moving the arms in a wide arc from above the sternum to the sides, producing a deep stretch and concentric squeeze of the pectorals.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Low Fly
exercises.notes: Start standing with feet hip- to shoulder-width apart, knees soft and a slight hinge at the hips (standing, slight forward lean).
instructions: Start standing with feet hip- to shoulder-width apart, knees soft and a slight hinge at the hips (standing, slight forward lean).
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip at or just above thigh level (low position). Keep the spine neutral and shoulders down.
Maintain a small, fixed bend in the elbows throughout the movement; do not lock the joints.
Exhale and raise the dumbbells in a wide arc up and inward, leading with the elbows so the hands meet or come close in front of the upper chest/shoulder level (low-to-high arc).
Pause briefly at the top and actively squeeze the pectorals while keeping the shoulders stable (avoid shrugging).
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back along the same arc to the starting low position under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, keeping tempo controlled and avoiding momentum or excessive back extension.
exercises.description: A standing low-to-high dumbbell fly that moves the weights in an upward arc from near the hips to in front of the chest, emphasizing the pectorals. Keep a slight bend in the elbows and squeeze the chest at the top of the movement.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a supine pressing exercise performed on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also working the triceps and anterior delts. The lifter lowers a barbell to the chest and presses it back to full arm extension under control.

exercises.name: Barbell Incline Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set an adjustable bench to a 30-45° incline and position it inside a power rack or with a spotter and place the barbell on the rack at a height you can reach while seated.
instructions: Set an adjustable bench to a 30-45° incline and position it inside a power rack or with a spotter and place the barbell on the rack at a height you can reach while seated.
Sit with your back and head firmly against the bench, feet flat on the floor, and brace your core (tighten abs and glutes) to maintain a stable, slight arch in the lower back.
Grip the barbell with a pronated (overhand) grip slightly wider than shoulder-width; wrists stacked over elbows and shoulders pulled down and back.
Unrack the bar by straightening your arms and moving the bar directly above your upper chest/shoulder line; have a spotter if needed.
Inhale, bend the elbows, and lower the bar under control to the upper chest/clavicle area (touch point just below the top of the sternum) keeping elbows at roughly 30–60° from the torso.
Pause briefly at the bottom without bouncing, ensuring the forearms remain vertical and the core stays braced.
Exhale and press the bar back up in a slightly backward arc until the arms are fully extended but not locked aggressively at the elbows.
Repeat for the desired repetitions, then carefully re-rack the bar on the uprights with control or have the spotter assist.
exercises.description: The barbell incline bench press is a compound pressing movement performed on an inclined bench that emphasizes the upper portion of the pectorals while also engaging the anterior delts and triceps. It is done from a supine position on an incline bench, pressing a barbell vertically from chest to full arm extension.

exercises.name: Cable Upper Chest Crossovers
exercises.notes: Set both cable pulleys to a high position (above shoulder height) and attach single-handle grips.
instructions: Set both cable pulleys to a high position (above shoulder height) and attach single-handle grips.
Stand centered between the stations with a staggered or shoulder-width stance for stability and grab a handle in each hand (palms facing down or slightly rotated), arms out to the sides.
Hinge slightly forward at the hips so your torso is upright but leaning a bit toward the floor; keep chest up, shoulders down and back, and a slight bend in the elbows throughout.

Exhale and pull both handles in a controlled downward and inward arc, bringing your hands together in front of the upper chest/clavicle area (hands may cross slightly) while actively squeezing the pectorals.
Pause briefly at the peak contraction, keeping the torso stable and avoiding shoulder rotation or shrugging.
Inhale and slowly return the handles along the same arc back to the start position with control, resisting the weight rather than letting it pull you back.
Keep core engaged and avoid using momentum; choose a weight that allows full range of motion with good form.
exercises.description: Standing cable crossovers performed with the pulleys set high to emphasize the upper chest; the movement brings the handles down and together in a downward arc to target the clavicular portion of the pectorals. Maintain a slight forward lean and a small bend in the elbows while squeezing the chest at the finish.

exercises.name: Barbell Wide Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set up a flat bench under a rack and load the barbell; position yourself lying supine on the bench with eyes roughly under the bar.
instructions: Set up a flat bench under a rack and load the barbell; position yourself lying supine on the bench with eyes roughly under the bar.
Plant feet firmly on the floor, pull your shoulder blades together and down (retract and depress) and maintain a tight core and neutral spine.
Grip the bar with a wide, pronated grip (wider than shoulder-width) so the forearms are more angled when the bar is at chest level.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar over your chest, keeping shoulders stable and the elbows under the bar.
Inhale, slowly lower the bar in a controlled manner to the mid-chest / nipple line while keeping the elbows tracking out at a moderate flare and the scapulae retracted.
Pause briefly with the bar lightly touching the chest (do not bounce), then exhale and press the bar back up along a slight arc until the arms are nearly extended.
Avoid locking the elbows aggressively at the top, maintain tension in the pectorals and triceps, and keep the wrists neutral.
Return the bar to the rack with control after your set; use a spotter if lifting heavy or approaching failure.
exercises.description: A barbell bench press performed with a wider-than-shoulder grip to emphasize the outer pectoral fibers. Lying supine on a flat bench, the bar is lowered to the mid-chest and pressed back to full arm extension.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Fly
exercises.notes: Set an incline bench to about 30-45 degrees and sit with a dumbbell in each hand resting on your thighs.
instructions: Set an incline bench to about 30-45 degrees and sit with a dumbbell in each hand resting on your thighs.
Lie back (supine on an incline bench) and plant your feet flat on the floor for stability; bring the dumbbells up to shoulder height with palms facing each other and a slight bend in the elbows.
Retract and depress your shoulder blades slightly (pinch the shoulder blades together) to stabilize the chest and protect the shoulders.
Begin the repetition by slowly lowering the dumbbells out to your sides in a wide arc, keeping the same slight elbow bend throughout; lower until you feel a comfortable stretch in the upper chest.
Keep the movement controlled and avoid dropping the weights below the plane of the chest or allowing the elbows to fully lock or flare excessively.
Drive the dumbbells back up by horizontally adducting the shoulders (bringing the arms together) until they meet or nearly meet above the sternum; exhale as you lift.
Pause briefly at the top with tension in the pectorals, then inhale and repeat for the desired reps.
Maintain a neutral neck and avoid shrugging the shoulders; use a weight that allows strict form and full control through both eccentric and concentric phases.
exercises.description: Performed lying back on an inclined bench, the dumbbell incline fly isolates the upper portion of the chest by moving the arms in a wide arc from open to together while maintaining a slight elbow bend. It emphasizes pectoral horizontal adduction with shoulder stabilization from the delts and grip support from the forearms.

exercises.name: Cable Low Fly
exercises.notes: Set both cable pulleys to the lowest position and attach single-handle grips; select a moderate weight.
instructions: Set both cable pulleys to the lowest position and attach single-handle grips; select a moderate weight.
Stand centered between the towers in a staggered or shoulder-width stance with knees soft and core braced (slight forward lean at the hips).
Reach down and grab each handle with a neutral or slightly supinated grip, palms facing in or up, elbows slightly bent and fixed in that position throughout the movement.
Begin with the arms out to the sides and low (handles near hips or knees) and inhale.
Exhale and pull the handles upward and inward in a wide arc, keeping the elbows slightly bent, bringing the hands together or in front of the chest/upper sternum.
Pause briefly at the peak contraction and actively squeeze the pectorals while avoiding excessive shoulder shrugging.
Slowly reverse the motion under control, allowing the cables to return the arms to the start position while maintaining tension in the chest.
Repeat for desired repetitions and re-rack safely; keep torso stable and avoid using momentum.
exercises.description: Standing cable low fly is a low-to-high cable chest fly that targets the pectorals by sweeping the arms upward and together from low pulley positions; performed standing with a slight forward lean and a soft bend in the elbows to emphasize the chest contraction.

exercises.name: Cable Low Fly
exercises.notes: Set both cable pulleys to the lowest position and attach single-hand handles.
instructions: Set both cable pulleys to the lowest position and attach single-hand handles.
Stand centered between the towers in an upright position with feet shoulder-width apart or one foot slightly staggered for stability.
Grasp the handles with a neutral grip so the cables run low behind your legs; step forward slightly to create tension in the cables and keep a tall chest.
Keep a slight bend in the elbows throughout the exercise (soft elbows) and a small forward lean from the hips; do not round the shoulders.
Exhale and pull both handles upward and inward in a controlled wide arc until your hands meet or nearly meet at chest level, squeezing the pectorals at the end of the motion.
Hold the peak contraction briefly, keeping the shoulders down and scapulae stable (use serratus anterior to stabilize the shoulder blades).
Inhale and slowly reverse the movement, allowing the cables to return your arms to the start position with control — do not let the weights slam.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining posture, a consistent elbow angle, and steady tempo throughout.
exercises.description: Standing cable low fly performed with the pulleys set low; you pull each handle upward and inward in a wide arc to bring the hands together in front of the chest, emphasizing horizontal adduction to work the pectorals. Maintain a slight elbow bend and controlled tempo throughout the movement.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Fly
exercises.notes: Set an adjustable bench to a 30–45° incline and sit with a dumbbell in each hand resting on your thighs.
instructions: Set an adjustable bench to a 30–45° incline and sit with a dumbbell in each hand resting on your thighs.
Lie back so your head, upper back and glutes are supported (supine on an incline bench) and place your feet flat on the floor for stability.
Use your legs to help rack the dumbbells and press them overhead so your arms are extended above your chest, palms facing each other (neutral grip); keep a slight bend in the elbows.
Retract your shoulder blades slightly and maintain a neutral spine throughout the movement; avoid excessive lower-back arching.

Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells in a wide, controlled arc out to your sides until you feel a stretch in the upper chest—stop when your elbows are roughly in line with the bench or slightly below shoulder level.
Keep the elbows fixed at the same slight bend throughout the descent; do not let the elbows drop or the wrists bend excessively.
Exhale and bring the dumbbells back together over your chest by squeezing the pectorals, following the same arc path used on the way down.
Pause briefly at the top with the weights close together (but avoid slamming them), then repeat for the desired number of reps.
Use a lighter weight if you feel shoulder joint pain; focus on controlled range of motion and chest contraction rather than heavy load.
exercises.description: Lying on an incline bench, perform a wide-arc dumbbell fly to emphasize the upper portion of the chest; movement stretches the chest as you lower and contracts the pectorals as you bring the weights back together. Keep a slight bend in the elbows and move in a controlled arc to avoid shoulder strain.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip- to shoulder-width apart and the barbell over mid-foot; use an overhand (pronated) grip slightly wider than shoulder width.
Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees until your torso is roughly 45 degrees to parallel to the floor; keep the spine neutral (no rounding) and engage the abs and glutes.
Hold the bar with arms extended and shoulders protracted, chest up and eyes looking a few feet in front of you to maintain a neutral neck.
Initiate the rep by driving the elbows back and up, pulling the bar toward your lower chest / upper abdomen while squeezing the shoulder blades together.
Keep the elbows close to the body (not flaring excessively) and avoid using momentum from the hips; breathe out as you pull.
Lower the bar slowly and under control to the starting position with arms extended while maintaining the hip hinge and neutral spine; breathe in on the descent.
Repeat for the desired reps, controlling tempo and keeping the torso stable — if you cannot maintain a flat back, reduce weight or stop and reset.
exercises.description: Standing hip-hinged barbell row where you pull a loaded barbell toward the lower chest/upper abdomen; primarily targets the lats and upper back while requiring posterior chain and core stabilization. Maintain a flat back and drive the elbows back to retract the shoulder blades.

exercises.name: Cable Seated Row with V-bar
exercises.notes: Attach the V-bar to the low pulley and grasp it with a neutral grip (palms facing each other).
instructions: Sit on the bench facing the cable machine with feet braced against the footplate and knees slightly bent.
Attach the V-bar to the low pulley and grasp it with a neutral grip (palms facing each other). Sit upright with a neutral spine and chest lifted.
Start with your arms extended and shoulders relaxed forward — feel a slight stretch in the lats.
Exhale and initiate the movement by driving your elbows straight back, keeping them close to your sides.
Retract and squeeze the shoulder blades together and pull the V-bar to the lower chest/upper abdomen; avoid excessive torso lean or using momentum.
Hold and squeeze the contracted position for 1 second, focusing on the lats and upper back.
Inhale and slowly extend your arms forward under control until the lats are stretched and you return to the start position.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, maintaining a neutral spine and controlled tempo throughout.
exercises.description: Seated cable row with a V-bar is a horizontal pulling exercise that emphasizes the lats and middle back by pulling the handle toward the torso while seated. It uses a neutral grip to allow a strong elbow-driven pull and a full contraction of the back muscles.

exercises.name: Lever Shrug (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Load the plate-loaded lever machine to the desired weight and stand facing forward with feet hip-width apart, toes pointing straight ahead.
instructions: Load the plate-loaded lever machine to the desired weight and stand facing forward with feet hip-width apart, toes pointing straight ahead.
Grasp the machine handles with a neutral (palms facing the body) grip and let your arms hang fully extended at your sides; maintain a neutral spine and soft knees.
Keep your torso upright and immobile; do not lean or use leg drive throughout the movement.
Exhale and elevate (shrug) your shoulders straight up toward your ears by driving the scapulae upward—think of trying to touch your shoulders to your ears.
Hold the top position and squeeze the traps and levator scapulae for 1–2 seconds while keeping the arms straight and grip firm (forearms engaged).
Inhale and slowly lower the shoulders back down to the starting position under control until the traps are fully stretched.
Repeat for the prescribed repetitions; avoid rolling or rotating the shoulders and avoid excessive neck extension—keep your head neutral and eyes forward.
exercises.description: Standing plate-loaded lever shrugs target the upper trapezius by elevating the shoulders while holding the machine handles; the lift is a vertical scapular elevation with a controlled lowering phase.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

Hold the bar with arms extended and shoulders protracted, chest up and eyes looking a few feet in front of you to maintain a neutral neck.
Initiate the rep by driving the elbows back and up, pulling the bar toward your lower chest / upper abdomen while squeezing the shoulder blades together.
Keep the elbows close to the body (not flaring excessively) and avoid using momentum from the hips; breathe out as you pull.
Lower the bar slowly and under control to the starting position with arms extended while maintaining the hip hinge and neutral spine; breathe in on the descent.
Repeat for the desired reps, controlling tempo and keeping the torso stable — if you cannot maintain a flat back, reduce weight or stop and reset.
exercises.description: Standing hip-hinged barbell row where you pull a loaded barbell toward the lower chest/upper abdomen; primarily targets the lats and upper back while requiring posterior chain and core stabilization. Maintain a flat back and drive the elbows back to retract the shoulder blades.

exercises.name: Cable Seated High Row (V-bar)
exercises.notes: Adjust the cable pulley to a high position and attach the V-bar.
instructions: Adjust the cable pulley to a high position and attach the V-bar. Sit on the bench facing the machine with feet flat on the floor and knees bent.
Grasp the V-bar with a neutral (palms facing) grip and sit upright with a tall chest, slight natural lumbar curve, and shoulders relaxed downward.
Start with your arms extended overhead (or up in front) so there is tension on the cable and your shoulder blades are not fully protracted.
Exhale and pull the V-bar down and back by driving your elbows toward your ribcage and slightly behind you, focusing on squeezing your shoulder blades together (scapular retraction).
Hold the contracted position briefly (1–2 seconds) with your torso stable and not leaning back excessively.
Inhale and slowly control the V-bar back to the start position, allowing the arms to extend while maintaining core tension and posture.
Repeat for the desired reps. Keep movements smooth, avoid jerking or using momentum, and do not let the lower back round or overextend.
exercises.description: Seated cable high row using a V-bar is a horizontal/upper-angle pulling movement that targets the muscles of the upper back by driving the elbows down and back while squeezing the shoulder blades together. It uses a neutral grip to emphasize middle traps, rhomboids and lats with assistance from the arms.

exercises.name: Lever Front Pulldown
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat and thigh pads so your feet are flat on the floor and your thighs are secured under the pads when seated upright.
instructions: Adjust the seat and thigh pads so your feet are flat on the floor and your thighs are secured under the pads when seated upright.
Sit upright (not reclined) with a neutral spine, grasp the overhead handles with a pronated (palms facing away) or neutral grip depending on the machine, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Begin with your arms extended overhead and your shoulder blades elevated; brace your core and keep chest up.
Exhale and pull the handles down in a smooth arc toward the upper chest by driving your elbows down and back, focusing on squeezing the lats and pinching the shoulder blades together.
Avoid excessive torso lean or using momentum; keep the movement controlled and lead with the elbows rather than pulling with the hands.
Pause briefly at the bottom with the handles near your chest while maintaining scapular retraction and lat tension.
Inhale and slowly return the handles to the start position with a controlled motion until your arms are extended overhead and your shoulder blades are slightly apart.
Repeat for the desired reps. Use a weight that allows full, controlled range of motion and maintain steady breathing throughout (exhale pulling down, inhale returning up).
exercises.description: Seated lever front pulldown performed on a machine where you pull handles from overhead down in front of you; it primarily targets the lats while engaging the upper back and arm muscles. Maintain a tall torso and lead the movement with the elbows to maximize back activation.

exercises.name: Lever Bent over Row (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Set the lever machine or plate-loaded bar to the desired weight and stand with feet hip-width under the bar/platform.
instructions: Set the lever machine or plate-loaded bar to the desired weight and stand with feet hip-width under the bar/platform.
Hinge at the hips with a soft bend in the knees until your torso is approximately 30–45 degrees from vertical (bent-over position); keep the spine neutral and neck in line with the torso.
Grasp the bar with an overhand (or chosen) grip about shoulder-width apart and let your arms hang straight down; engage your core to stabilize the trunk.
Initiate the rep by pulling your elbows back and driving the bar toward your lower chest/upper abdomen, squeezing the shoulder blades together at the top of the movement.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining a braced torso, then slowly lower the bar back to the start under control until the arms are extended.
Repeat for the desired reps, avoiding torso rotation, excessive momentum, or rounding of the lower back throughout the set.
exercises.description: A bent-over lever row performed from a hip-hinged position to drive the loaded bar toward the torso, emphasizing the mid/upper back. It develops pulling strength and upper-back thickness while also engaging the arms and rear shoulders.

exercises.name: Barbell Back Wide Shrug
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart and knees slightly bent.
instructions: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart and knees slightly bent.
Place a loaded barbell across the back of your hips/upper thighs or behind your body and take a wide, overhand grip (hands wider than shoulder-width).
Keep your chest up, core braced, neck neutral and arms straight (no elbow bend) throughout the movement.
Raise (shrug) your shoulders straight up toward your ears as high as possible, focusing on contracting the traps—do not roll the shoulders.
Pause briefly at the top while squeezing the traps.
Slowly lower the shoulders back down to the starting position under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining a stable torso and avoiding forward/backward lean.
exercises.description: Standing barbell shrug performed with a wide, behind-the-back grip to place emphasis on the upper traps; elevate the shoulders straight up toward the ears while keeping the arms extended and torso stable.

exercises.name: Cable Pushdown (straight arm)
exercises.notes: Attach a straight bar, rope, or single handle to a high pulley.
instructions: Attach a straight bar, rope, or single handle to a high pulley. Select a moderate weight that allows controlled movement while keeping arms mostly straight.
Stand facing the machine with feet hip-width apart (or a slight stagger for balance). Grasp the handle with both hands (pronated or neutral grip) and step back slightly so the cable is taut.

Hinge slightly forward at the hips so your torso leans about 10–20 degrees; keep knees soft and maintain a neutral spine. Arms should be extended in front of you with a small, fixed bend in the elbows (not fully locked).

Initiate the rep by driving the shoulders into extension: press the hands downward and back in a controlled arc until the hands reach thigh level and you feel maximal contraction in the lats. Keep the elbows stationary and avoid bending them to pull.
Pause briefly at the bottom while squeezing the lats and maintaining straight-arm tension.
Slowly return the handle to the starting position under control, keeping constant tension on the cable and avoiding letting the shoulders fully relax at the top.
Breathe out during the downward (working) phase and inhale while returning to the start. Perform desired number of reps with steady tempo.
exercises.description: Standing straight-arm cable pushdowns target the lats by driving the arms down from a high cable with minimal elbow flexion. The movement emphasizes shoulder extension and scapular retraction while keeping the torso stable.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.notes: Light weight, focus on form and range of motion

warmup.notes: Brief warmup to activate target muscles. Can skip if first working set feels warm.

exercises.name: Cable one arm lat pulldown
exercises.notes: Setup: Attach a single D-handle to the high pulley and sit facing the machine on the seat with knees secured under the pads; sit upright with feet flat on the floor.
instructions: Setup: Attach a single D-handle to the high pulley and sit facing the machine on the seat with knees secured under the pads; sit upright with feet flat on the floor.
Grip and position: Reach up and grasp the handle with a neutral or overhand grip using one hand; keep the torso tall with a slight backward lean from the hips and brace your core.

Start position: Allow the arm to be fully extended overhead while maintaining scapular stability (shoulder blades slightly down and back), and keep the opposite hand resting or lightly stabilizing the thigh.
Pull: Exhale and pull the handle down and back by driving your elbow toward your hip/side of the torso, focusing on squeezing the lats and retracting the shoulder blade.
Peak contraction: Pause briefly at the bottom when your hand reaches about chest/hip level and you feel a strong contraction in the lats; avoid rotating the torso or shrugging the shoulder.
Return: Inhale and slowly control the handle back up to the fully extended start position, resisting the weight to maintain tension throughout the eccentric phase.
Repetitions and switch: Perform the desired number of repetitions on one side, then switch to the other arm to maintain balance.
exercises.description: Seated single-arm cable pulldown uses a high pulley and single handle to pull one arm down and back, isolating the lats while encouraging unilateral strength and balance. The movement emphasizes pulling the elbow toward the hip/side of the torso with a controlled tempo.

exercises.name: Cable Wide Grip Rear Pulldown Behind Neck
exercises.notes: Set the lat pulldown machine with a wide bar attachment and select an appropriate weight; sit on the bench and lock your thighs under the pads with feet flat on the floor.
instructions: Set the lat pulldown machine with a wide bar attachment and select an appropriate weight; sit on the bench and lock your thighs under the pads with feet flat on the floor.
Reach up and grasp the bar with a wide, overhand grip (palms facing away) with hands wider than shoulder-width.
Sit tall with a slight lean back at the hips, brace your core, and allow your arms to start fully extended overhead.

Initiate the movement by pulling your elbows down and back, driving the bar behind your head toward the upper trapezius/base of the skull while leading with the elbows and squeezing the shoulder blades together.
Pause briefly at the bottom with the shoulder blades fully retracted, then slowly and under control allow the bar to return to the starting position until the arms are fully extended.
Breathe out while pulling the bar down and inhale while returning; avoid jerking, using momentum, or forcing the bar too far behind the neck to reduce shoulder strain.
Use a moderate weight and stop the exercise if you experience shoulder or neck pain; consider using a front pulldown variation if you have limited shoulder mobility.
exercises.description: Seated cable wide-grip pulldown where the bar is pulled down behind the neck to emphasize the upper portion of the lats and upper back. The movement uses a wide overhand grip and scapular retraction to drive the descent.

exercises.name: Lever T-bar Row (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Set up the lever T-bar station: load plates on the bar, position the foot platform, and select the handle attachment.
instructions: Set up the lever T-bar station: load plates on the bar, position the foot platform, and select the handle attachment. Stand on the platform with feet about hip-width apart.

Hinge at the hips and slightly bend the knees so your torso is angled forward (roughly 30–45 degrees) while keeping a neutral spine and chest up; grip the handles with a double overhand or neutral grip.
Brace your core and retract your shoulder blades before initiating the lift to maintain a stable torso throughout the movement.
Exhale and pull the handle toward your lower chest/upper abdomen by driving the elbows back and squeezing the lats and mid-back, keeping elbows relatively close to the body.
Pause briefly at the top with scapulae squeezed and chest slightly lifted, avoiding excessive leaning back or shrugging the shoulders.
Inhale and lower the handle under control until the arms are nearly fully extended and you feel a stretch in the lats, maintaining the hip hinge and neutral spine.
Repeat for the desired reps. Keep tempo controlled, avoid using momentum, and maintain the hinged standing position throughout the set.
exercises.description: A plate-loaded lever T-bar row is a bent-over rowing movement performed from a hinged standing position that primarily targets the lats while engaging the upper back and pulling muscles. It involves pulling a landmine-style bar/handle toward the torso to develop thickness and strength through the mid-back.

exercises.name: Cable High Row with Chest Support
exercises.notes: Set two cable pulleys to a high position and attach single-hand or D handles; choose a moderate weight.
instructions: Set two cable pulleys to a high position and attach single-hand or D handles; choose a moderate weight.

Position an incline bench or chest pad in front of the machine so you can sit facing the pulleys with your chest pressed firmly against the pad and feet flat on the floor; adjust pad height so torso is supported and arms can reach the handles overhead.
Sit tall with chest against the pad, neutral neck, core braced and shoulders down away from ears; reach up and grasp the handles with a neutral or overhand grip.
Begin with arms nearly extended overhead, scapulae slightly protracted; inhale to prepare.
Exhale and pull the handles down and back toward the upper chest by driving your elbows down and out, leading with the elbows and squeezing the shoulder blades together at the end range.
Hold the contracted position briefly (1–2 seconds) while maintaining a supported chest and no torso rotation.
Slowly release the handles under control, allowing the arms to extend back to the start while keeping tension on the cables and scapulae engaged.
Repeat for desired reps; avoid using momentum, leaning away from the pad, or shrugging the shoulders upward.
exercises.description: Seated chest-supported cable high row performed from dual high pulleys; you pull the handles down and back toward the upper chest while the chest rests against the pad to isolate the upper back. Emphasizes scapular retraction and posterior shoulder activation with reduced lower-back involvement.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Shrug
exercises.notes: Stand upright (feet about hip-width), hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip at your sides, knees softly bent and core braced.
instructions: Stand upright (feet about hip-width), hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip at your sides, knees softly bent and core braced.
Keep your arms straight (no shrugging with the elbows) and maintain a neutral spine and head position throughout.
Exhale and lift (shrug) both shoulders straight up toward your ears as high as you can, focusing on contracting the top of the traps.
Pause and squeeze at the top for 1–2 seconds, avoiding rolling or rotating the shoulders.
Inhale and slowly lower the shoulders back down to the starting position with control until arms are fully relaxed at your sides.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, keeping movement vertical and controlled; increase weight only if you can maintain form.
exercises.description: Standing exercise using dumbbells to raise the shoulders toward the ears, primarily working the trapezius muscles to develop upper-back and neck strength. Performed with a short vertical shrugging motion while keeping the arms straight.

exercises.name: Cable seated row
exercises.notes: Initiate the movement by retracting your shoulder blades, then drive your elbows straight back keeping them close to your body while pulling the handle toward your lower sternum/upper abdomen.
instructions: Sit on the bench facing the cable machine with feet braced on the footplate and knees slightly bent (seated, upright position).
Grab the handle (V-bar or close grip) with a neutral or overhand grip and extend your arms so the weight stack is lifted and shoulders are protracted; maintain a neutral spine and chest slightly up.
Initiate the movement by retracting your shoulder blades, then drive your elbows straight back keeping them close to your body while pulling the handle toward your lower sternum/upper abdomen.
Keep the torso stable—avoid excessive leaning or using momentum; maintain a slight forward trunk angle only if required to reach the handle, then return to a tall, braced posture as you pull.

Squeeze the shoulder blades and lats at the end range for a brief 1–2 second pause, then slowly extend the arms forward under control to the start position while allowing the scapulae to move forward.
Breathe out while pulling and inhale while returning to the start; choose a weight that allows controlled form and full elbow extension between reps.
exercises.description: Seated cable row is a horizontal pulling exercise performed sitting with feet braced that primarily targets the lats while working the middle/upper back and arm pulling muscles. It emphasizes scapular retraction and elbow drive to pull the handle to the torso.

exercises.name: Cable Seated Row with V-bar
exercises.notes: Attach the V-bar to the low pulley and grasp it with a neutral grip (palms facing each other).
instructions: Sit on the bench facing the cable machine with feet braced against the footplate and knees slightly bent.
Attach the V-bar to the low pulley and grasp it with a neutral grip (palms facing each other). Sit upright with a neutral spine and chest lifted.
Start with your arms extended and shoulders relaxed forward — feel a slight stretch in the lats.
Exhale and initiate the movement by driving your elbows straight back, keeping them close to your sides.
Retract and squeeze the shoulder blades together and pull the V-bar to the lower chest/upper abdomen; avoid excessive torso lean or using momentum.
Hold and squeeze the contracted position for 1 second, focusing on the lats and upper back.
Inhale and slowly extend your arms forward under control until the lats are stretched and you return to the start position.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, maintaining a neutral spine and controlled tempo throughout.
exercises.description: Seated cable row with a V-bar is a horizontal pulling exercise that emphasizes the lats and middle back by pulling the handle toward the torso while seated. It uses a neutral grip to allow a strong elbow-driven pull and a full contraction of the back muscles.

exercises.name: Cable Wide Grip Lat Pulldown
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat height and knee pads so your thighs are secured under the pads when seated.
instructions: Adjust the seat height and knee pads so your thighs are secured under the pads when seated.
Select an appropriate weight and grasp the wide bar with a pronated (overhand) grip wider than shoulder width.
Sit tall with a slight natural arch in the lower back, feet flat, chest up and head neutral.
Begin the rep by inhaling, then exhale as you pull the bar down by driving your elbows down and back toward your ribcage.
Lead the motion with your elbows and squeeze your shoulder blades together at the bottom, bringing the bar to the upper chest or collarbone level.
Pause briefly while maintaining tension in the lats, keeping the torso relatively upright and avoiding excessive backward lean or swinging.
Inhale and slowly allow the bar to return to the start position with controlled extension of the arms until you feel a full stretch in the lats.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining steady tempo and controlled breathing throughout.
exercises.description: Seated cable wide-grip lat pulldown using an overhand wide bar to pull the cable down toward the upper chest, emphasizing shoulder adduction and scapular depression to work the latissimus dorsi. Performed seated with knees braced to isolate the back and minimize leg drive.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Squat
exercises.notes: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height.
instructions: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height. Position yourself under the bar so it rests across your upper traps (high-bar) or rear delts (low-bar) depending on preference.
Grip the bar with hands just outside shoulder width, elbows pointing down and chest up. Stand with feet roughly shoulder-width apart and toes pointed slightly outward.
Brace your core and take a full breath in, then unrack the bar by extending your legs and taking 1–2 controlled steps backward to clear the rack; establish a stable, shoulder-width stance.
Keep a neutral spine and an upright chest. Initiate the movement by pushing your hips back slightly and bending at the knees, sending your knees outward to track over your toes.
Descend in a controlled manner until your thighs are at least parallel to the floor (or deeper if mobility allows) while keeping weight on your midfoot/heels and maintaining a braced core.
Avoid knee collapse (valgus) — consciously push knees outward and keep toes engaged. Do not let the lower back round; maintain a neutral spine throughout.
Drive through your heels and midfoot to stand back up, exhaling as you ascend and extending hips and knees simultaneously until fully upright.
Reset your breathing and bracing between reps as needed. When finished, step forward under control and re-rack the bar securely.
Use appropriate weight, a spotter or safety pins in the rack if lifting near maximum loads, and adjust stance or bar position to reduce discomfort in shoulders or lower back.
exercises.description: Barbell back squat is a compound, standing lower-body exercise that primarily develops the quadriceps while also engaging the glutes, hamstrings and core for stability. The barbell rests across the upper back while you descend by bending hips and knees, then drive back up to standing.

exercises.name: Barbell Romanian Deadlift
exercises.notes: Stand upright holding a barbell with an overhand (or mixed) grip, feet about hip-width apart and the bar resting against your thighs; maintain a neutral spine and engaged core.
instructions: Stand upright holding a barbell with an overhand (or mixed) grip, feet about hip-width apart and the bar resting against your thighs; maintain a neutral spine and engaged core.
Set a small, consistent bend in the knees (soft knees) — do not bend into a squat; this is a hinge at the hips, not a knee-dominant lift.
Take a breath in, brace your core, then push your hips back while keeping the chest up and spine neutral; allow the bar to travel down close to the front of your legs.
Lower the bar until you feel a strong but safe stretch in the hamstrings (typically to mid‑shin), keeping the bar close to the body and shoulders pulled back — avoid rounding the lower back.
Pause briefly at the bottom while maintaining tension in the hamstrings and back; keep your head in a neutral alignment (looking a few feet in front of you).
Exhale and drive your hips forward by contracting the glutes and hamstrings to return to the standing start position; do not hyperextend the lower back at the top.
Control the descent and ascent on every rep, using a weight that lets you maintain a neutral spine and full hip hinge pattern.
If grip is limiting, switch to a mixed grip or use straps; always prioritize form over load and stop if you feel lumbar rounding or sharp pain.
exercises.description: A bilateral hip-hinge performed from standing that emphasizes the hamstrings by lowering a loaded barbell along the thighs with a neutral spine and slight knee bend; returned to standing by driving the hips forward and squeezing the glutes. Builds posterior chain strength and improves hip extension.

exercises.name: Barbell Standing Leg Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Set up a squat rack at an appropriate height and load the barbell; unrack the bar and place it securely across your upper back/traps (not on your neck).
instructions: Set up a squat rack at an appropriate height and load the barbell; unrack the bar and place it securely across your upper back/traps (not on your neck).

Step onto a raised, stable platform or aerobic step so the balls of your feet are on the edge and your heels can drop freely; stand upright with feet about hip-to-shoulder width and toes pointing forward.
Grip the bar with a comfortable overhand grip to maintain control; engage your abs and keep your chest up and spine neutral throughout (standing position).
Keep knees straight but not locked (a slight soft bend) and maintain weight balanced over the balls of your feet.
Exhale and raise your heels by plantarflexing at the ankle, lifting as high as possible onto your toes and pausing 1–2 seconds at the top to maximize contraction.
Inhale and slowly lower your heels down below the platform level until you feel a stretch in the calves; control the descent — do not drop quickly or bounce.
Repeat for the desired repetitions, maintaining a steady tempo and full range of motion. Use a spotter or safety pins in the rack if using heavy weight.
When finished, carefully step down from the platform and re-rack the bar in the squat rack.
exercises.description: Standing barbell calf raise performed from a raised platform with a barbell across the upper back; rise onto the toes to concentrically contract the calves, then lower the heels below the platform for a full stretch. This is a standing, weight-bearing isolation exercise for the calves with upper-back and core stabilization.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.description: Barbell back squat is a compound, standing lower-body exercise that primarily develops the quadriceps while also engaging the glutes, hamstrings and core for stability. The barbell rests across the upper back while you descend by bending hips and knees, then drive back up to standing.

exercises.name: Barbell Low Bar Squat
exercises.notes: Set the barbell in a squat rack at mid-chest height and position yourself under the bar so it rests on the rear delts / just below the spine of the scapula (low-bar position).
instructions: Set the barbell in a squat rack at mid-chest height and position yourself under the bar so it rests on the rear delts / just below the spine of the scapula (low-bar position).
Grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder-width, retract the shoulder blades and push the elbows back to create a stable shelf across the upper back; stand to unrack the bar.
Step back from the rack with feet about shoulder-width to slightly wider, toes pointed slightly outward; establish a stable, flat-footed stance.
Take a deep breath and brace your abs and upper back tight to create full-body tension before descent.
Begin the descent by sending the hips back and down while allowing the knees to track outward over the toes; keep the chest up and the spine neutral.
Lower until thigh crease is around parallel to the floor or your comfortable depth while maintaining tension and a consistent bar path over mid-foot.
Drive upward by pressing through the heels and mid-foot, extending hips and knees simultaneously while keeping the core braced and the upper back stable.
Exhale/finish the rep at full hip and knee extension, reset your brace as needed, then repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
When finished, step forward under control and rack the bar safely on the supports.
exercises.description: A barbell low-bar squat places the bar lower across the rear delts/upper back and emphasizes a hip-dominant squat pattern while still loading the thighs. The exercise trains knee and hip extension by lowering to squat depth and driving back up through the heels.

exercises.name: Barbell Hip Thrust
exercises.notes: Setup a flat bench behind you and sit on the floor with your upper back (around the scapulae) resting against the bench edge; roll a barbell over your legs and position it across your hips (use a pad if needed).
instructions: Setup a flat bench behind you and sit on the floor with your upper back (around the scapulae) resting against the bench edge; roll a barbell over your legs and position it across your hips (use a pad if needed).
Plant your feet flat on the floor about hip-width apart so that at the top of the movement your knees are roughly bent to 90 degrees; keep chin tucked and gaze forward or slightly up.
Brace your abs and grip the barbell on each side to steady it; maintain a neutral spine (avoid overarching the lower back).
Drive through your heels and contract the glutes to lift your hips upward until your torso and thighs form a straight line (hips fully extended); squeeze the glutes at the top for 1–2 seconds.
Lower the hips slowly and under control until they are just above the floor or until you feel a stretch in the glutes/hamstrings, maintaining core tension throughout.
Repeat for the desired reps, keeping movement smooth and controlled and avoiding thrusting with the lower back.
If needed, adjust foot distance to emphasize glutes (feet slightly forward) or hamstrings (feet further from bench) while preserving a stable foot contact and neutral spine.
exercises.description: The barbell hip thrust is a supine, bench-supported hip extension exercise that primarily develops the glutes by driving the hips upward against resistance. It also recruits the hamstrings and quads while the core stabilizes the trunk.

exercises.name: Lever Lying Leg Curl
exercises.notes: Set the lever machine so the pivot aligns with the knee joint and place the lower-leg pad just above the heels / over the Achilles area.
instructions: Set the lever machine so the pivot aligns with the knee joint and place the lower-leg pad just above the heels / over the Achilles area.
Lie prone (face down) on the bench with your hips flat on the pad, chest supported, and hands holding the front handles or bench for stability.
Position your legs fully extended with the pad resting on the back of your lower legs and feet relaxed; maintain a neutral spine throughout.
Exhale and curl your lower legs by flexing the knees, bringing your heels toward your glutes in a controlled motion until you reach near-full knee flexion.
Pause and squeeze the hamstrings at the top for 1 second, keeping hips and torso pressed into the bench (avoid lifting the hips).
Inhale and slowly lower the lever back to the start (near full knee extension) under control without letting the weight slam or locking the knees.
Repeat for the desired reps, maintaining steady tempo and full range of motion; adjust load so you can control both concentric and eccentric phases.
Stop or reduce weight if you feel excessive hip rise, lower-back strain, or knee pain; keep movements smooth and joint-focused.
exercises.description: Prone (lying face down) leg curl performed on a lever machine that isolates the hamstrings by flexing the knee against a padded lever. The movement curls the lower leg toward the buttocks while the torso remains stabilized on the bench.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Calf Raise (plate loaded)
exercises.notes: Place the balls of your feet on the edge of the foot platform with heels hanging freely off the platform.
instructions: Sit on the seated calf raise machine so your knees are bent at about 90 degrees and your back is upright (seated position).
Place the balls of your feet on the edge of the foot platform with heels hanging freely off the platform.
Position the padded lever across your thighs just above the knees and load the desired plates on the lever (plate-loaded).
Grasp the handles or machine grips for stability and brace your core.
Begin with your heels lowered until you feel a stretch in the calves (ankle dorsiflexion).
Drive through the balls of your feet to raise your heels as high as possible (ankle plantarflexion), lifting the lever and plates.
Pause and squeeze your calves at the top for 1–2 seconds while keeping knees bent and torso stable.
Lower your heels slowly and under control back to the starting stretched position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, avoiding bouncing or using hip/knee extension to assist the movement.
exercises.description: Seated, plate-loaded calf raise that isolates the calf musculature by plantarflexing the ankles while the knees are bent; the lever pad rests on the thighs and weight is applied via a plate-loaded lever. Performed from a seated position to emphasize the soleus and overall calf development.

exercises.name: Lever Rotary Calf
exercises.notes: Sit upright on the machine with your back against the pad and knees bent at about 90 degrees; adjust the thigh pad so it rests firmly on your thighs.
instructions: Sit upright on the machine with your back against the pad and knees bent at about 90 degrees; adjust the thigh pad so it rests firmly on your thighs.
Place the balls of your feet on the footplate with heels hanging off the edge; grip the handles at your sides for stability.
Start with your ankles dorsiflexed (heels lowered) to feel a stretch in the calves.
Exhale and press down through the balls of your feet to plantarflex the ankles, raising your heels as high as comfortably possible while maintaining control.
If the machine allows rotary motion, rotate the footplate slightly inward or outward across sets or reps to emphasize medial or lateral calf fibers while maintaining the same plantarflexion pattern.
Pause briefly at the top for maximal contraction, then inhale and slowly lower your heels back to the starting stretched position with a controlled tempo.
Keep the movement smooth, avoid bouncing, and maintain a stable seated posture (do not lift hips or lock knees).
exercises.description: Seated lever rotary calf is a seated plantarflexion exercise performed on a lever/rotary calf machine that isolates the calf muscles through a full range of motion, often allowing a slight rotary/angle change at the footplate to target different calf fibers.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Squat to Overhead Press
exercises.notes: Start standing with feet about hip-to-shoulder width, toes pointing slightly out; hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height (rack position) with elbows under the weights and palms facing each other or forward.
instructions: Start standing with feet about hip-to-shoulder width, toes pointing slightly out; hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height (rack position) with elbows under the weights and palms facing each other or forward.

Brace your core, keep a neutral spine and chest up; inhale and begin the squat by pushing your hips back and bending your knees, lowering until your thighs are approximately parallel to the floor (or slightly below if mobile).
Keep your knees tracking over your toes and weight distributed through your heels and midfoot; avoid letting the knees collapse inward.
Pause briefly at the bottom while keeping tension in the quads and glutes.
Exhale and drive through your heels to stand up explosively, extending the hips and knees until you reach full standing position.

As you complete the stand, press the dumbbells overhead by extending your elbows and driving through the shoulders until arms are fully extended overhead; finish with the weights roughly in line with your ears.
Control the descent: lower the dumbbells back to shoulder/rack position, then hinge the hips and descend into the next squat to begin the next rep.
Maintain a braced core and neutral spine throughout; breathe in on the descent (squat) and exhale as you stand and press. Use a weight that allows controlled movement and safe technique.
exercises.description: A compound standing movement that combines a dumbbell squat followed immediately by an overhead press, training the thighs while also working the shoulders and upper body. It builds lower-body strength and power while challenging core stability and pressing strength.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Romanian Deadlift to Bent Over Row
exercises.notes: Stand tall holding a dumbbell in each hand with feet hip-width apart and palms facing the body (neutral grip).
instructions: Stand tall holding a dumbbell in each hand with feet hip-width apart and palms facing the body (neutral grip).

Brace your abs, keep a slight bend in the knees, and hinge at the hips by pushing them back to lower the dumbbells along the front of your thighs until you feel a stretch in the hamstrings (torso will lean forward but keep a neutral spine).
Stop the descent when the dumbbells reach about mid-shin or when you can no longer maintain a flat back; shoulders should be packed and shoulder blades slightly retracted.

From this hinged position, initiate the bent-over row by pulling the dumbbells toward your lower ribs/hips, driving the elbows up and back and squeezing the shoulder blades together (keep torso stable and hips fixed).
Lower the dumbbells under control back to the hanging position at the end of the RDL, maintaining the hip hinge.
To return to standing, drive the hips forward while keeping the core braced and extend the hips until you are upright with the dumbbells at your sides.
Repeat the sequence: hinge (Romanian deadlift) then row, keeping movements smooth, spine neutral, and avoiding excessive rounding of the back or using momentum.
exercises.description: A compound combo that starts with a Romanian deadlift hip hinge to load the hamstrings and glutes, then transitions to a bent-over dumbbell row to work the lats and upper back. Maintains a neutral spine throughout while moving from the hinge to the rowing position.

exercises.name: Cable Donkey Diagonal Kickback
exercises.notes: Attach an ankle cuff to the low cable pulley and secure it around the working ankle.
instructions: Attach an ankle cuff to the low cable pulley and secure it around the working ankle.
Stand facing the cable machine and place your hands on the frame or a handle at chest height for support; keep the non-working leg slightly bent and weight-bearing.
Hinge forward slightly at the hips so your torso is angled, brace your abs, and keep your spine neutral (avoid arching or rotating the torso).
Start with the working knee bent approximately 90° and the foot close to the floor; this is the start position.
Exhale and drive the working heel back and up in a diagonal line (backward and slightly outward/upward) by contracting the glute, keeping the knee bent throughout the motion.
Pause and squeeze the glute at the top of the movement while maintaining a neutral pelvis and preventing hip rotation.
Inhale and slowly return the leg back to the start position under control, keeping tension on the cable.
Repeat for the desired reps, then switch legs. Keep movements smooth and controlled; avoid using momentum or excessive lumbar extension.
exercises.description: A cable-resisted donkey-style kick performed on a diagonal plane to emphasize the glute muscles (particularly the posterior and lateral fibers) while limiting lumbar involvement. The movement drives the heel/back of the foot up and back in a controlled diagonal arc against cable resistance.

exercises.name: Smith Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Set the Smith machine bar to a comfortable shoulder height and place two sturdy blocks or a raised platform under the bar so your heels can hang freely off the edge when standing on the platform.
instructions: Set the Smith machine bar to a comfortable shoulder height and place two sturdy blocks or a raised platform under the bar so your heels can hang freely off the edge when standing on the platform.

Load the bar with an appropriate weight and step under it, placing the bar across your upper back (traps/shoulders) or hold it securely with straight arms at hip level, feet hip-width apart on the platforms with toes pointing forward.
Stand tall with a neutral spine, knees straight but not locked (a slight bend), and shoulders back to stabilize the torso.
Begin the repetition by slowly lowering your heels down below the platform edge until you feel a stretch in the calves (eccentric phase).
Drive through the balls of your feet and raise your heels as high as possible, contracting the calves at the top (concentric phase); pause briefly and squeeze.
Lower your heels under control back to the stretched position; avoid bouncing or using momentum.
Repeat for the desired number of reps and, when finished, carefully rack the bar and step down from the platforms.
exercises.description: Standing Smith machine calf raise performed on raised platforms to allow a full heel drop and a full plantarflexion at the top; primarily trains the calf muscles while the lower body stabilizers assist. The movement is done with the bar held on the shoulders or hands while the heels move up and down off the edge of the platform.

exercises.name: Lever Leg Extension (Inward Toes)
exercises.notes: Sit upright on the leg extension machine with your back firmly against the back pad and adjust the seat so the machine's knee pivot lines up with your knee joint.
instructions: Sit upright on the leg extension machine with your back firmly against the back pad and adjust the seat so the machine's knee pivot lines up with your knee joint.
Position the lower pad just above your ankles and place your feet on the footpad with your toes turned inward (feet slightly internally rotated). Grip the side handles or seat for stability.
Start with knees bent at about 90 degrees (or the machine's start position). Brace your core and keep your spine neutral; do not arch your lower back.
Exhale and extend your knees, pushing the pad upward until your legs are nearly straight but avoid locking the knees at full extension.
Pause briefly at the top, maintaining tension in the quads, then inhale and lower the weight slowly and with control back to the start position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining the inward toe position and steady tempo throughout. Stop if you feel sharp knee pain and ensure the movement is smooth and controlled.
exercises.description: Seated leg extension performed on a lever/machine with the toes turned inward; the movement isolates knee extension to target the quadriceps while the inward toe position slightly alters quad activation. The torso stays seated against the back pad and the lower shins press into the machine pad as you extend and lower the legs under control.

exercises.name: Lever One Leg Extension
exercises.notes: Adjust the machine seat so your back is flat against the backrest and your knee aligns with the machine's pivot point; place the lower shin pad just above the ankle of the working leg.
instructions: Adjust the machine seat so your back is flat against the backrest and your knee aligns with the machine's pivot point; place the lower shin pad just above the ankle of the working leg.
Sit upright with your non-working leg resting relaxed, grip the side handles for stability, and brace your core.
Start with the working knee bent at about 90 degrees and the foot under the pad.
Exhale and extend the working leg by straightening the knee until it is nearly locked out but avoid full hyperextension; pause 1–2 seconds at the top.
Inhale and slowly lower the weight back to the start position with controlled tension, keeping the movement smooth.
Complete the desired reps on one leg, then switch to the other leg; maintain an upright posture and avoid using momentum or swinging the torso.
exercises.description: A single-leg seated lever extension that isolates the quadriceps by extending the knee against resistance on a leg extension machine. Performed one leg at a time to improve quad strength and muscular balance.

exercises.name: Lever Leg Extension
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat so your back is fully supported and the knee joint aligns with the machine's pivot; place the lower shin pad just above the ankles.
instructions: Adjust the seat so your back is fully supported and the knee joint aligns with the machine's pivot; place the lower shin pad just above the ankles.
Sit upright with feet under the roller pad, grip the side handles or seat edges for stability, and keep your chest lifted and core engaged.
Start with knees bent to about 90 degrees (or the machine's starting position) and inhale to prepare.
Exhale and extend your legs by straightening the knees until they are almost fully extended — do not lock the knees at the top.
Pause briefly (0.5–1 second) at the top to feel the contraction in the quads.
Inhale and slowly lower the lever back to the starting position with a controlled eccentric motion until knees return to the initial bend.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions while keeping the torso still and avoiding momentum or bouncing.
exercises.description: Seated leg extension performed on a lever machine that isolates the quadriceps by extending the knees against a padded lever. The exercise focuses on controlled knee extension and eccentric lowering to target the front of the thighs.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Leg Extension
exercises.notes: Adjust the seat and backpad so your knees align with the machine's pivot point and your back is supported (knees should start around 90° flexion).
instructions: Adjust the seat and backpad so your knees align with the machine's pivot point and your back is supported (knees should start around 90° flexion).
Set the lower shin pad so it rests just above your ankles when seated; grip the handles or seat edges for stability.
Sit upright with your chest up, core engaged, and feet under the pad (seated position).
Exhale and extend your knees by pressing the pad forward with your lower shins until your legs are nearly straight; avoid hyperextending or locking the knees.
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining tension in the quads.
Inhale and slowly lower the weight back to the start position with controlled motion until your knees return to approximately 90°.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, keeping the torso stable and avoiding momentum or swinging.
exercises.description: Seated leg extension on a lever-style machine isolates and strengthens the quadriceps by extending the knee against resistance while seated. The torso stays braced against the backpad and the lower shin pushes the pad forward until the knee is nearly straight.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.
